title: Blue Dragon freezes target on successful effect roll

card.rawText: When this creature hits, roll 1 die. If the result is 4-6, your target is frozen for 1 turn cycle and cannot inflict Atk damage or be sacrificed.

plan.title: Blue Dragon freezes target on successful effect roll
plan.summary: Verify ON_HIT opens effect roll and 4-6 applies Frozen for 1 turn cycle with damage/sacrifice flags disabled.

notes: Use a post-first-turn-cycle state so combat damage is allowed.
Blue Dragon should be player_1 primary; Dire Wolf player_2 primary.

steps: Start battle with Blue Dragon attacking Dire Wolf.
Resolve hit roll as a hit.
manualVerification: Confirm the Effect Roll modal appears after hit and before damage roll.
Advance one full turn cycle and confirm Frozen expires at the configured start-player turn boundary.
riskNotes: Path names for status flags may differ in engine snapshots.

coverageSuggestion.notes: Supported route; test both success and later a failure roll 1-3.

review.summary: The observed result matches the core Blue Dragon freeze-on-hit plan: ON_HIT was detected, an effect roll was created after the hit and before damage, forced roll 4 succeeded, FROZEN was applied to the hit target with canInflictAtkDamage=false and canBeSacrificed=false, and the target's retaliation was skipped due to the status.
evidence: Event 8 BATTLE_TIMING_TRIGGER_DETECTED shows Blue Dragon effect 001-E01 triggered on ON_HIT.
Event 11 EFFECT_ROLL_CREATED targets player_2's Owl God and states success on 4-6.
Event 13 EFFECT_ROLL_ROLLED shows dice [4] with success=true.
Event 14 EFFECT_ROLL_APPLIED applies status FROZEN with flags canInflictAtkDamage=false and canBeSacrificed=false and duration amount 1 turn cycle.
Player_2 primaryCreature activeStatuses contains FROZEN from Blue Dragon with flags canInflictAtkDamage=false and canBeSacrificed=false.
Event 17 BATTLE_STRIKE_SKIPPED_BY_STATUS confirms the frozen creature could not inflict attack damage.
suspectedIssues: The plan setup named Dire Wolf as the defender, but the match used Owl God. This does not invalidate the effect behavior but reduces exact fixture fidelity.

Event 9 EFFECT_RUNTIME_TRACE lists the target as Blue Dragon/player_1 before EFFECT_ROLL_CREATED correctly targets Owl God/player_2; this may be a trace/logging inconsistency rather than a gameplay issue.
The plan's manual verification to advance one full turn cycle and confirm expiration was not covered in the provided summary.
suggestedNextSteps: Run the same test with the intended Dire Wolf defender if exact fixture matching is required.
Add/inspect a follow-up expiration check at player_1 turn start #2 to verify Frozen cleanup.
Review EFFECT_RUNTIME_TRACE target fields for consistency with the actual effect roll target.

coverageSuggestion.notes: Core success-path application and attack-damage prevention are covered. Expiration after one full turn cycle and sacrifice prevention via an attempted sacrifice were not directly verified.

runnerNotes: Create a dev match from plan.setup, execute plan.steps, then verify plan.expectedAssertions.
The LLM only proposes the fixture. The deterministic engine and manual QA status remain source of truth.
